name: Marvel’s Spider-Man Remastered
description: In Marvel’s Spider-Man Remastered, the worlds of Peter Parker and Spider-Man collide in an original action-packed story. Play as an experienced Peter Parker, fighting big crime and iconic villains in Marvel’s New York. Web-swing through vibrant neighborhoods and defeat villains with epic takedowns.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS: Windows 10 64-bit
  • Processor: Intel Core i3-4160, 3.6 GHz or AMD equivalent
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GTX 950 or AMD Radeon RX 470
  • DirectX: Version 12
  • Storage: 75 GB available space
comment1:
[h1]A few thoughts on Marvel’s Spider-Man Remastered. [/h1]

My 4-year-old son is a huge Spider-Man fan, and since the game was basically dirt cheap during a Steam sale, I picked it up thinking we’d just mess around with it a little on weekends.

We started casually: TV, Xbox controller, full chill mode. He would move from mission to mission, swing around the city and explore, while I handled most of the combat. It quickly turned into a surprisingly fun father-son gaming experience.

Then at some point I realized... I was getting seriously hooked myself and started playing even when he wasn’t around, because the game is genuinely insanely fun.

Long story short: between playing together and me continuing solo, we almost 100%-ed the game and all the DLCs (we’re only missing 3 suits), and somehow ended up spending around 50 hours in it.

And yes, “we’ll just try it for a bit” remains the biggest lie in gaming right after “just one more quest.”

I had heard the Spider-Man games were great, but honestly, I didn’t expect them to be [b]THIS [/b] good. Especially considering the original game came out in 2018. From the movement and combat to the animations and the overall feeling of New York City, everything is crafted with an absurd amount of care and attention to detail.

Huge respect to Insomniac Games. They absolutely nailed it.

If you enjoy action-adventure games and somehow still haven’t played this gem, I highly recommend it. The game is absolutely worth your time.

Also, we’re already about 2.5 hours into Marvel's Spider-Man: Miles Morales and that game already feels like a proper next step instead of just a reskin of the first one. Which honestly makes me even more excited to eventually get to Marvel’s Spider-Man 2.

But one thing at a time. Right now, me and my son are busy trying to beat Miles Morales challenges. :)
comment2:
winging around New York alone makes this game worth playing.
The combat feels smooth, the story hits harder than expected, and visually it still looks amazing. Some side activities get repetitive, but honestly, being Spider-Man never stops being fun.

One of the best superhero games ever made. 🎮🕷️
comment3:
Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered was an amazing experience from start to finish. The web-swinging around New York City never got old, the combat felt smooth and satisfying, and the story was genuinely engaging with some memorable emotional moments. The graphics still look fantastic, and the PC version runs very well overall.

I also went for the 100% achievement completion, which gave me even more reasons to explore everything the game has to offer. While I enjoyed completing all the content, some of the achievements felt a little repetitive since they required doing similar activities multiple times. It wasn't difficult, just a bit grindy toward the end.

The game isn't completely perfect either. I encountered a few bugs during my playthrough, such as occasional visual glitches and minor issues with NPCs, but nothing serious enough to ruin the experience.

Overall, Marvel's Spider-Man Remastered is one of the best superhero games I've played. Despite a few bugs and some repetitive achievement requirements, the fantastic gameplay, great story, and incredible traversal make it an easy recommendation.
